sections.paragraph:
, חֲמֹר [“once f. 2Sa 19:27 ”], m.-

(1) an ass, Gen 49:14 Exo 13:13 so called from the reddish colour, which in southern countries belongs not only to the wild ass, but also to the common or domestic ass; from which it is called in Spanish, burro, burrico. Comp. also צָחַר.

(2) i.q. חֹמֶר a heap; this more rare form is perhaps employed on account of the paronomasia. Jud. 15:16 , בִּלְחִי הַחֲמֹור חֲמֹור חֲמֹורָתַיִם “with the jawbone of an ass (I have killed) a heap, (even) two heaps.” Root חָמַר No. 3.

(3) [Hamor], pr.n. of a Hivite, a cotemporary of the patriarchs, Gen 33:19 34:2 Jos 24:32 Jud. 9:28 .
